disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No lip/tongue/facial swelling, no wheeze/breathlessness, no hives, normal breathing (no anaphylaxis)
trigger_question_examples: Any swelling of your lips, tongue or face, wheeze, or trouble breathing?
Any raised itchy welts (hives), or tightness in your throat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No swelling of my lips or face, no wheeze, my breathing is fine, and no hives.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rash blanches on glass test; patient is afebrile and well (not meningococcaemia)
trigger_question_examples: When you press a glass firmly on the spots, do they fade (blanch) or stay?
Do you have a fever or feel very unwell?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They fade when I press a glass on them, and I don't have a fever — I feel okay in myself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Teardrop papules with silvery scale erupting 1-2 weeks after streptococcal pharyngitis; family history of an autoimmune skin condition
trigger_question_examples: What did the spots look like — teardrop-shaped with a fine scale?
Did this follow your throat infection, and does anyone in the family have an autoimmune skin condition?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, teardrop-shaped with fine scale, a week or so after the strep throat, and my parent has an autoimmune skin condition.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this a penicillin allergy? Am I about to have a serious reaction?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen anaphylaxis features first; if absent and the rash blanches with a classic post-strep teardrop pattern, explain this is a post-infectious scaly rash (an immune reaction to the throat infection), not a drug allergy or meningococcal rash, and provide emollient/topical steroid with anaphylaxis and non-blanching-fever safety-nets — without ED over-triage.
